name: VIX (CBOE Volatility Index)
summary: Created 2026-05-13 per markets dossier E1 promotion. **C9 VIX 17.26 (-3.4% 1D, -5.0% 1M, -14.9% 3M) — CYCLE LOW SINCE WAR BEGAN FEB 28; PAPER-PHYSICAL BIFURCATION AT EXTREME.** Expected on a day with two new kinetic incidents (Hui Chuan + Haj Ali), an Israeli KIA inside the Litani trigger window, Trump Iran ultimatum overnight ('no longer tolerate'), and Iranian Army spokesperson Akraminia 'surprising options' rhetoric: VIX should be 22-25. Got: 17.26. **Why complacency**: Trump-Xi joint statement read as tail-risk removal; S&P/NASDAQ/Dow at record closes May 14 → MoM gamma drives systematic vol selling; pre-NVDA earnings May 20 positioning bullish risk; VIX ETF complex pushing contango trade aggressively; foreign safe-haven USD bid is the alternative hedge of choice — VIX is being short-circuited as the crisis-hedge instrument. **CLEANEST PAPER-PHYSICAL BIFURCATION READING OF THE CYCLE**: Paper assets are pricing structural-stability + tail-risk-removed + AI-cycle dominance. Physical complex (Brent +1.6% refusing to fall, VLCC TD3C still multi-week highs, kinetic incidents continuing, war risk insurance still ~8x pre-war) is pricing intact-blockade + escalation-tail-risk. **ONE SIDE RESOLVES VIOLENTLY WITHIN 5-10 SESSIONS.** Asymmetric setup: if Trump-Xi joint statement produces no operational follow-through by May 20 and Brent breaks $108 sustained 2 sessions, VIX has 5-7 points of repricing upside; if Hormuz reopens physically, VIX could compress further to 15. PRIOR (May 14): VIX 17.87 sub-18 on 1-week-clock-close day; summit-pause digital regime. **G4 VIX 17.87 (-0.7% 1D, -2.7% 1M, -13.3% 3M) — SUB-18 ON A 1-WEEK-CLOSE DAY** = on Day 7/7 of Trump's 'decide within a week' mandate, with Brent above $105 and Hormuz at 2-5 ships/day, VIX should print 22-24. Sub-18 says markets fully priced Trump-Xi pause through May 16. **If pause fails to deliver framework, VIX +30% spike to 23-24 by Monday is the base case** (markets dossier G4 + H). Trump-Xi summit Day 1: Trump publicly opened HEU-via-China custodian door; Vance softer line; Pentagon Sledgehammer naming. Forced-pause-via-Beijing hypothesis operationally validated through clock close. **Per markets dossier H reversibility map: VIX 12-14 (-22 to -33%) if MOU signs by May 21; VIX 26-32 (+45 to +80%) if Hormuz remains closed through June.** Binary digital — summit is forcible time-window where the binary collapses to a digital. PRIOR (May 13): VIX has emerged as the cleanest transmission mechanism for binary event-window pricing in the cycle — appearing in 4+ causal chains (C1, C2 direct; C5, C9 indirect). The summit-pause mechanic operationalises VIX as a digital around discrete event windows. **C1 VIX 17.99 (-2.1% 1D, -5.9% 1M, -13.6% 3M) = THE PARADOX OF MAY 13** — expected on day of Trump-rejection-1-week-clock-close + Litani-crossing + 90%-enrichment-rhetoric + Saudi-strikes-disclosure + USD/INR record: VIX +5-10% intraday. Got: -2.1%, below May 6 post-pause low, approaching pre-war territory. **Why: Trump-in-Beijing summit (May 14-15) makes a kinetic strike order operationally impossible through May 15-16. Markets are not pricing the threat universe; they are pricing the FORCIBLE DELAY of decision. Options-positioning desk reads: 48-hour vol kill via timeline mechanics.** Additional drivers: EIA forecast cut to $94.85 (TASS) institutional vote against Brent fear premium; Aramco/ADNOC quiet limited Hormuz resume (World Oil May 8) confirms physical supply ~90% preserved through East-West routing; Iran missile fire rate -92% from peak (JPost + CNN intel) = capability-vs-willingness gap publicly quantified. **Investor implication is asymmetric: if Trump returns from Beijing without an order, VIX 16-handle is plausible and Brent retraces to $97-100. If he returns AND orders, VIX +30% intraday and Brent $115-120. The summit is a binary digital, not a continuous variable. The market is positioned long-vol-cheap into a known event window.** Reversibility: total — VIX 17.99 prices NO escalation through the summit + modest probability beyond; a single tweet from Beijing or a single missile launch reprices everything within the same session. VVIX (vol-of-vol) sitting at low band; pre-CPI hedges unwinding after digestion was orderly. **VIX behaviour now central to graph because it operationalises the kinetic vs paper-disruption-vs-de-escalation trinary in a tradeable way** — the band $15-25 maps to the three branches, and the summit creates a forcible time-window where the binary collapses to a digital.

current.price: 17.26 (May 15 — CYCLE LOW since war began Feb 28; down from 17.87 May 14)

Markets fully priced Trump-Xi joint statement as tail-risk removal; S&P/NASDAQ/Dow record closes May 14 driving systematic vol selling. **One side resolves violently within 5-10 sessions. Asymmetric setup**: no Trump-Xi operational follow-through by May 20 + Brent breaks $108 sustained 2 sessions = VIX 5-7 points repricing upside; Hormuz reopens physically = VIX 15.

active_triggers.condition: VIX <16 sustained 3 sessions = de-escalation confirmation marker
VIX >25 intraday = kinetic-event marker (Trump strike order, Iran ballistic launch, Israeli enrichment strike)
VIX +30% intraday move within 48h of Trump's return from Beijing (May 15-16)
active_triggers.note: Per markets dossier C1: summit is binary digital; Trump returns either with kinetic order (VIX +30% / Brent $115-120) or without (VIX 16-handle / Brent $97-100).

On Day 7/7 of Trump's 'decide within a week' mandate with Brent above $105 and Hormuz at 2-5 ships/day, VIX should print 22-24. Got -2.1%, below May 6 post-pause low, approaching pre-war territory. **Why: Trump-in-Beijing structural ceiling — summit makes kinetic strike order operationally impossible through May 15-16; markets pricing FORCIBLE DELAY of decision, not threat universe; options-positioning desk reads 48-hour vol kill via timeline mechanics.** Additional drivers: EIA forecast cut to $94.85 (institutional vote against Brent fear premium); Aramco+ADNOC quiet limited Hormuz resume (World Oil May 8) confirms physical supply ~90% preserved through East-West routing; Iran missile fire rate -92% from peak (JPost+CNN intel).
